name: Mitra
description: Mitra, a significant deity in both the Hindu and Zoroastrian traditions, embodies the concepts of contracts, oaths, friendship, and alliances. His name, derived from the Proto-Indo-Iranian root *mitra-, signifies 'contract' or 'agreement,' highlighting his crucial role as the divine guarantor of truth and fidelity. In the Rigveda, Mitra is frequently invoked alongside Varuna, forming a dual divinity (Mitra-Varuna) that oversees cosmic order and upholds moral law. While Varuna represents the overarching sovereignty and cosmic justice, Mitra focuses on the practical application of these principles within human relationships, ensuring the sanctity of promises and the harmonious functioning of society. He is often associated with the sun and light, symbolizing clarity and openness in dealings. Later Vedic texts and Puranic literature further elaborate on Mitra's attributes, solidifying his position as a benevolent deity who promotes peace, cooperation, and mutual respect. His enduring presence across diverse cultures underscores the universal importance of trust and integrity in maintaining social cohesion.
subtitle: The God of Contracts and Friendship

keyMyths.title: Mitra and Varuna's Cosmic Watch
keyMyths.description: Mitra and Varuna, often appearing together, are depicted as watchful guardians of the cosmos. They oversee the actions of mortals and gods alike, ensuring adherence to the established order (rita). Their combined power is formidable; Mitra's focus on agreements complements Varuna's cosmic sovereignty, creating a balanced system of justice and order. They ride in a golden chariot, observing all that transpires, and punish those who break their oaths or violate the divine law. This myth emphasizes the importance of integrity and accountability.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Friendship
keyMyths.description: While not explicitly detailed in a single narrative, Mitra's very essence embodies the creation and maintenance of friendship. He is considered the divine personification of amicable bonds and mutual understanding. Ritual offerings and prayers directed to Mitra are believed to strengthen existing friendships and forge new, trustworthy alliances. His presence is invoked in ceremonies involving oaths and contracts, ensuring the participants are bound by a sense of loyalty and reciprocal obligation. These rituals reinforce the societal value of cooperation and trust.

keyMyths.title: Mitra as a Solar Deity
keyMyths.description: Although sometimes debated, Mitra is associated with the sun, particularly with the light of day. This association links him to clarity, truth, and the exposure of falsehood. As the sun shines, revealing all that is hidden, Mitra ensures that contracts are transparent and agreements are honored. The sun's daily journey across the sky mirrors Mitra's constant vigilance, overseeing the interactions and commitments of individuals and communities. This solar connection reinforces his role as a guardian of integrity and a symbol of unwavering truth.

sources.author: Jan Gonda
sources.work: Die Religionen Indiens, I. Veda und älterer Hinduismus
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Vedic religion, including a detailed analysis of Mitra's role and significance.
sources.text: A comprehensive overview of Vedic religion, including a detailed analysis of Mitra's role and significance.

sources.author: Fritz Stolze
sources.description: Explores the historical and cultural context of Mitra worship in Persia, highlighting its connection to Zoroastrianism.
sources.text: Explores the historical and cultural context of Mitra worship in Persia, highlighting its connection to Zoroastrianism.

sources.author: Georges Dumézil
sources.work: Mitra-Varuna: An Essay on Two Indo-European Representations of Sovereignty
sources.description: A seminal work examining the dual divinity of Mitra and Varuna, analyzing their respective roles in maintaining cosmic and social order.
